tmc/perf.h File Reference

Support accessing perf counters from user space. More...


Data Structures

struct  tmc_perf_counters
 The counters are 32-bits, so for example, the TMC_PERF_ONE event counter will overflow in 3.5 seconds at 1.2 GHz. More...

Defines

#define TMC_PERF_NUM_COUNTERS   4
 Number of hardware performance counters.

Enumerations

enum  tmc_perf_events_e {
  TMC_PERF_INVALID, TMC_PERF_ZERO, TMC_PERF_ONE, TMC_PERF_PASS_WRITTEN,
  TMC_PERF_FAIL_WRITTEN, TMC_PERF_DONE_WRITTEN, TMC_PERF_SBOX_QUIESCED, TMC_PERF_CBOX_QUIESCED,
  TMC_PERF_TRACE_SAMPLE, TMC_PERF_CYCLE_12, TMC_PERF_CYCLE_8, TMC_PERF_CYCLE_4,
  TMC_PERF_ICOH, TMC_PERF_L1D_FILL_STALL, TMC_PERF_CBOX_FULL_STALL, TMC_PERF_LOAD_HIT_STALL,
  TMC_PERF_LOAD_STALL, TMC_PERF_ALU_SRC_STALL, TMC_PERF_IDN_SRC_STALL, TMC_PERF_UDN_SRC_STALL,
  TMC_PERF_MF_STALL, TMC_PERF_SLOW_SPR_STALL, TMC_PERF_NETWORK_DEST_STALL, TMC_PERF_INSTRUCTION_STALL,
  TMC_PERF_PFB_HIT_IN_PFB, TMC_PERF_PFB_HIT, TMC_PERF_CBOX_RESP, TMC_PERF_MEM_OP,
  TMC_PERF_CBOX_REQ, TMC_PERF_ITLB_MISS_INTERRUPT, TMC_PERF_ITLB_MISS_INT = TMC_PERF_ITLB_MISS_INTERRUPT, TMC_PERF_INTERRUPT,
  TMC_PERF_INT = TMC_PERF_INTERRUPT, TMC_PERF_ICACHE_FILL_PEND, TMC_PERF_ICACHE_FILL, TMC_PERF_WAY_MISPREDICT,
  TMC_PERF_COND_BRANCH_PRED_CORRECT, TMC_PERF_COND_BRANCH_PRED_INCORRECT, TMC_PERF_VALID_WB, TMC_PERF_INSTRUCTION_BUNDLE,
  TMC_PERF_RESTART, TMC_PERF_USED_RAS, TMC_PERF_RAS_CORRECT, TMC_PERF_COND_BRANCH_NO_PREDICT,
  TMC_PERF_TLB, TMC_PERF_READ, TMC_PERF_WRITE, TMC_PERF_TLB_EXCEPTION,
  TMC_PERF_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_WRITE_MISS, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_LD_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_ST_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_ATOMIC_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_FLUSH_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_INV_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_FINV_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_MF_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_PFETCH_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_WH64_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_DTLBPR_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_FWB_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_LD_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID, TMC_PERF_L1_OPCODE_ST_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_SNOOP_INCREMENT_READ, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_NON_INCREMENT_READ, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_WRITE, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_READ,
  TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_WRITE, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_DATA_READ, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_WRITE, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_INSTRUCTION_READ,
  TMC_PERF_REMOTE_DATA_READ, TMC_PERF_REMOTE_WRITE, TMC_PERF_REMOTE_INSTRUCTION_READ, TMC_PERF_COHERENCE_INVALIDATION,
  TMC_PERF_SNOOP_INCREMENT_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_NON_INCREMENT_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_WRITE_MISS, TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_READ_MISS,
  TMC_PERF_SNOOP_IO_WRITE_MISS, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_DATA_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_WRITE_MISS, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_INSTRUCTION_READ_MISS,
  TMC_PERF_REMOTE_DATA_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_REMOTE_WRITE_MISS, TMC_PERF_REMOTE_INSTRUCTION_READ_MISS, TMC_PERF_COHERENCE_INVALIDATION_HIT,
  TMC_PERF_CACHE_WRITEBACK, TMC_PERF_SDN_STARVED, TMC_PERF_RDN_STARVED, TMC_PERF_QDN_STARVED,
  TMC_PERF_SKF_STARVED, TMC_PERF_RTF_STARVED, TMC_PERF_IREQ_STARVED, TMC_PERF_ITLB_OLOC_CACHE_MISS,
  TMC_PERF_DTLB_OLOC_CACHE_MISS, TMC_PERF_LOCAL_WRITE_BUFFER_ALLOC, TMC_PERF_REMOTE_WRITE_BUFFER_ALLOC, TMC_PERF_ARB_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_MDF_WRITE, TMC_PERF_LDB_READ, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_ST_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_ATOMIC_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_FLUSH_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_INV_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_FINV_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_MF_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_PFETCH_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_WH64_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_FWB_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_ST_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_NOFIL_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_LD_NOFIL_NON_TEMPORAL_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_RDN_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_QDN_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_IO_READ_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_IO_WRITE_VALID,
  TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_I_STREAM_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_MDF_VALID, TMC_PERF_L2_OPCODE_IREQ_IV_VALID, TMC_PERF_UDN_PACKET_SENT,
  TMC_PERF_UDN_WORD_SENT, TMC_PERF_UDN_BUBBLE, TMC_PERF_UDN_CONGESTION, TMC_PERF_IDN_PACKET_SENT,
  TMC_PERF_IDN_WORD_SENT, TMC_PERF_IDN_BUBBLE, TMC_PERF_IDN_CONGESTION, TMC_PERF_RDN_PACKET_SENT,
  TMC_PERF_RDN_WORD_SENT, TMC_PERF_RDN_BUBBLE, TMC_PERF_RDN_CONGESTION, TMC_PERF_SDN_PACKET_SENT,
  TMC_PERF_SDN_WORD_SENT, TMC_PERF_SDN_BUBBLE, TMC_PERF_SDN_CONGESTION, TMC_PERF_QDN_PACKET_SENT,
  TMC_PERF_QDN_WORD_SENT, TMC_PERF_QDN_BUBBLE, TMC_PERF_QDN_CONGESTION, TMC_PERF_UDN_DEMUX_STALL,
  TMC_PERF_IDN_DEMUX_STALL, TMC_PERF_WATCH, TMC_PERF_BCST0, TMC_PERF_BCST1,
  TMC_PERF_BCST2, TMC_PERF_BCST3, TMC_PERF_PCNT0, TMC_PERF_PCNT1,
  TMC_PERF_AUX_PCNT0, TMC_PERF_AUX_PCNT1
}
 Event definitions. More...

Functions

int tmc_perf_enable_counters (void)
 Enable accessing perf counters from user space.
int tmc_perf_disable_counters (void)
 Disable accessing perf counters from user space.
int tmc_perf_setup_counters (enum tmc_perf_events_e event1, enum tmc_perf_events_e event2, enum tmc_perf_events_e event3, enum tmc_perf_events_e event4)
 Select four events from the enum below to initialize the hardware counters.
void tmc_perf_read_counters (struct tmc_perf_counters *counters)
 Read the four hardware counters without clearing them.
void tmc_perf_clear_counters (void)
 Clear the four hardware counters.
void tmc_perf_add_counters (struct tmc_perf_counters *counters)
 Accumulate counts from the four hardware counters without clearing them.
uint64_t tmc_perf_get_cpu_speed (void)
 Get the CPU speed, in hertz.

Detailed Description

Support accessing perf counters from user space.
